title: Wrong output to text file
body:
The following code:

```
integer fdebug=0, line_cnt=0;

initial begin
    if( fdebug==0 )
        fdebug=$fopen("main_io.log","w");
end

always @(posedge clk) begin
    if( rst ) begin
        line_cnt <= 0;
    end else if(cen6) begin
        if( ram_cs ) line_cnt <= line_cnt+1;
        if( ram_cs &&  cpu_rnw ) $fdisplay(fdebug,"%04X -> %02X - %d", A, ram_dout, line_cnt );
        if( ram_cs && !cpu_rnw ) $fdisplay(fdebug,"%04X <- %02X - %d", A, dout, line_cnt );
    end
end
```
Should produce a distinct line_cnt output for each line in the _main_io.log_ text file. In Modelsim, that's the case. But somehow in Verilator you get the first four lines printed three times:

```
effe <- 01 -           0
effd <- ac -           1
effd -> ac -           2
effe -> 01 -           3
effe <- 01 -           0
effd <- ac -           1
effd -> ac -           2
effe -> 01 -           3
effe <- 01 -           0
effd <- ac -           1
effd -> ac -           2
effe -> 01 -           3
e000 -> 00 -           4
e000 <- 55 -           5
e000 -> 55 -           6
e000 <- 00 -           7
e001 -> 00 -           8
```
Note that the number in the last column should always increase. Note that **the waveforms are correct** when checked in gtkwave. The waveforms do follow the expected behaviour. The problem seems to be related to the file output. The correct behavior is:

```
effe <- 01 -           0
effd <- ac -           1
effd -> ac -           2
effe -> 01 -           3
e000 -> 00 -           4
e000 <- 55 -           5
e000 -> 55 -           6
e000 <- 00 -           7
e001 -> 00 -           8
e001 <- 55 -           9
```

**_Can you attach an example that shows the issue?_**  
It's a convoluted setup. Let me just explain the issue in case something similar shows up.

**_What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?_**
Verilator 5.002 2022-10-29 rev v5.002

**_What OS and distribution are you using?_**
Linux once 5.4.0-132-generic #148-Ubuntu SMP Mon Oct 17 16:02:06 UTC 2022 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux

**_May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?_**
Let me assist only in providing bug information rather than software development
